name: the-last-lecture
description: Randy Pausch's The Last Lecture — a life wisdom and legacy-building toolkit from a Carnegie Mellon professor dying of pancreatic cancer, about achieving childhood dreams, overcoming brick walls, enabling others' dreams, humility, gratitude, and living meaningfully when time is running out.
useCase: Learn how to live a meaningful life, achieve childhood dreams, persist through obstacles, and leave a legacy — from a dying professor's final lecture.
knownRisks: Pausch's story is emotionally powerful and may be difficult for those who have lost someone to cancer
His success story is exceptional — persistence alone does not guarantee dream achievement
The 'just positive thinking' tone may feel dismissive to those facing serious hardship
